ocr_findings.detail: Q12-4: Raw OCR choices order was ア=Sun-FB+Mon&Tue-inc, イ=Sun-FB+Tue-inc, ウ=Mon&Tue-inc, エ=Tue-inc, with printed answer key 'ア'. The cleaned stage REORDERED choices to ア=Sun-FB, イ=Sun-FB+Tue-inc, ウ=Sun-FB+Mon&Tue-inc, エ=Tue-inc, but KEPT the answer letter 'ア'. The MD now publishes Answer='ア．日曜日のフルバックアップファイル' (only Sunday FB) — which is semantically WRONG for restoring to Tuesday EOD when using incremental backups (you need Sunday-FB + Mon-inc + Tue-inc). The JSON has answer_index=2 (→ ウ = Sun-FB+Mon&Tue-inc, the correct exam answer) so JSON and MD DISAGREE on the answer. Release-impacting: learners reading the MD will memorize a wrong answer.

Q12-6 stem in cleaned/output is semantically corrupted: 'セルD4にE2による式計算式を求め、セルD4にE2の人金する式計算式は、セルD5、E4及びE5に複製して使うものとする。' The raw OCR stem was clear: 'セルD2とE2に設定した2種類の仮の消費税率でセルA4とA5の商品の税込み価格を計算するために、セルD4に入れるべき計算式はどれか。'. The cleaned stage corrupted the stem; output preserves the corruption in all three languages so the question becomes incomprehensible to learners.

Q12-5 raw OCR choices are 'ア.2 イ.3 ウ.4 エ.5' with answer エ (=5). The JSON output preserves these correctly with answer_index=3→'エ.5'. However the MD output and the cleaned stage have choices 'ア.2 イ.4 ウ.5 エ.6' with answer 'エ.6'. MD and JSON disagree on choice values, and MD disagrees with the raw OCR. Published MD answer value is therefore wrong (6 vs OCR's 5).
Q12-5 stem references B2~C8 (7 students, D2-D8) but cleaned table includes 8 rows (rows 2-9). Likely upstream OCR row-detection error; harmless to the question's intent but reduces fidelity.

Q12-6 cleaned table shows 消費税8%/消費税10% (0.08/0.10, prices 540/550/648/660) but raw OCR shows 0.1/0.2 (prices 550/600/660/720). Cleaned stage substituted plausible-looking real-world tax rates. Output-MD does not render the table so user-visible impact is reduced.

translation_findings.detail: Because Q12-6 JP stem is corrupted, ZH and EN translations faithfully reproduce gibberish. The original question requirement (calculate tax-inclusive prices using two tax rates) is lost in all three languages.
ZH/EN choice lists faithfully translate the reordered (and wrong-answer-mapped) JP choices for Q12-4, so the wrong-answer defect is replicated across all three languages.
